name: Grandma Sofie's Split Pea Soup with French Bread Croutons
recipeInstructions:
This is NOT low-fat, low-calorie, or low anything for that matter, but absolutely delicious and an all-time family favorite!
Rinse peas in cold water and bring to a boil in 3 quarts of water. Cut bacon small and brown with onion over medium heat. Add the onions and drippings to the peas and cook until the peas are soft and just starting to disintegrate, about one hour, stirring occasionally to prevent solids from settling out and sticking to the bottom of the pot.
Chop celery small and add with parsley to the soup and cook until the celery is soft, about 15 minutes more. Thin with water if necessary to achieve the desired consistency; it should be like light cream with little bits of pea still visible. If too thick, it resembles 'pea porridge' and may not be as appetizing.
Meanwhile, make the croutons by cutting the bread into 3/4-inch cubes (leave the crust on). Melt butter in a frying pan over medium heat until foam subsides but before the butter begins to brown. Add the bread cubes in batches sized according to your pan's size, ensuring they never exceed one layer deep.
Optional: sauté a small amount of garlic in the butter for added flavor. Fry the bread cubes until dark golden, stirring and tossing frequently to ensure even browning on all sides. Be patient; if the butter gets too hot, it may burn and become bitter. The croutons should be crisp, fairly dry, and light enough to float on top of the soup.
Serve the hot soup in bowls and pass the croutons in a basket for each person to add at the table. Note that you could use half a loaf of French bread for the croutons if making the soup alone. Otherwise, the croutons may disappear quickly!
